title: Ensure that 'Agentless scanning for machines' component  status is set to 'On'

input_row.title: Ensure that 'Agentless scanning for machines' component  status is set to 'On'
input_row.description: Using disk snapshots, the agentless scanner scans for installed software, vulnerabilities, and plain text secrets.
input_row.rationale: The Microsoft Defender for Cloud agentless machine scanner provides threat detection, vulnerability detection, and discovery of sensitive information.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From the Azure Portal Home page, select Microsoft Defender for Cloud 2. Under Management select Environment Settings 3. Select a subscription 4. Under Settings > Defender Plans, click Settings & monitoring 5. Under the Component column, locate the row for Agentless scanning for machines 6. Ensure that On is selected Repeat the above for any additional subscriptions.
input_row.remediation: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From the Azure Portal Home page, select Microsoft Defender for Cloud 2. Under Management select Environment Settings 3. Select a subscription 4. Under Settings > Defender Plans, click Settings & monitoring 5. Under the Component column, locate the row for Agentless scanning for machines 6. Select On 7. Click Continue in the top left Repeat the above for any additional subscriptions.
input_row.impact: Agentless scanning for machines requires licensing and is included in these plans: • Defender CSPM • Defender for Servers plan 2
input_row.default_value: By default, Agentless scanning for machines is off.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Open the AWS Management Console and navigate to the **AWS Systems Manager** service.
2. Under **Node Management**, select **Systems Manager Inventory**.
3. In the left pane, click on **Settings**.
4. Look for the section labeled **Agentless Scanning** and identify if it is enabled or not.
5. Verify that the **Agentless Scanning** for machines component is set to **On**. 
6. If it is not set to On, this indicates non-compliance, and you will need to take remediation steps.

**Expected secure configuration:**  
The **Agentless scanning** for machines should be enabled (set to **On**) to ensure that threats, vulnerabilities, and sensitive information can be effectively managed and detected.

**How to confirm compliance:**  
Review the **State** of the **Agentless scanning** setting; it should explicitly state that the status is "**On**". If it indicates otherwise or is absent, document the findings as non-compliant.
